seed: batch-04 P2; refutation of an intuitive-but-false belief (Ped-2, Ped-3)
passage: After a roulette wheel lands on red five times in a row, it feels like black is 'due'. This is the gambler's fallacy. Each spin is independent — the wheel has no memory — so the probability of black on the next spin is still about 48.6%, exactly what it was on the first spin. Teach against this: acknowledge the strong intuition, then refute it with independence.
